text:
How successful has Nato's strategy been in Afghanistan?
Under General David Petraeus, a major plank of Nato's International Security Assistance Force (ISAF) strategy has been "kill/capture raids" - lightning strikes on senior Taliban personnel to either take prisoner or kill.
But have they worked?
Isaf supplies no consistent data on the policy, other than issuing a string of press releases claiming success after success, releases which often describe several raids in different places simultaneously.
Researchers Alex Strick van Linschoten and Felix Kuehn wanted to find out exactly how the missions worked.
You can read their full report here.
Frustrated by the lack of hard data, Strick van Linschoten scraped the reports, then used the Tinderbox database package to process each incident and extract the key numbers.
Although, interestingly, there has been a decline in these raids since Petraeus left Afghanistan in July this year.
The report is a fund of data crucial to understanding the way Nato has fought the conflict.
We've mapped some of the key facts by province, too.
This data, painstakingly collated, is the first time we can get a real picture of what is happening.

Aside from occasional scraps thrown to the media by ISAF, this is the first time that we have been able to get a somewhat more nuanced picture of how ISAF is operating, minimum figures for how many people are being detained and killed as well as a makeshift way to evaluate the usefulness of ISAF's own aggregate numbers that supposedly show the successes of the raids in Afghanistan.
Strick van Linschoten has visualised the timeline of releases below - you can explore it by clicking and dragging - or seeing it on the original site.

For Minnesota, we have Wiggins in there for right now as his $<hide> seems a bit too low for me in what is a good match-up.
With T.J. Warren & Deandre Ayton out, Devin Booker has some great upside as he should be easily putting up <hide>+ shots tonight.
Kelly Oubre Jr. has been a solid producer since being acquired for the suns and should see some extra run with Warren out.
I think you can also go with some value with the Suns if you want to spend up elsewhere as Josh Jackson went off for a <hide>.<hide> DraftKings point performance last game and Dragan Bender has posted back-to-back double-doubles with both Holmes & Ayton out.
If Holmes is playing tonight, he is also someone to take a look at later.
For Portland, it's hard not to go with Jusuf Nurkic with how undersized the Suns are right now.
Nurkic's price has gone up a bit, but he has <hide>+ DraftKings point upside in this match-up.

As we are able to fit in two Centers with the UTIL spot in DraftKings, I did add Jahlil Okafor in our DraftKings lineup as he is coming off back-to-back <hide>+ DraftKings performances and between his low-price tag and the fact they'll need his size against OKC, I don't mind rolling the dice with him again tonight.

About <hide> civilians in Kachin State's Hpakant Township have reportedly been displaced by fresh clashes between the Burma Army and Kachin rebels in recent days.
RANGOON - About <hide> Kachin civilians in Kachin State's Hpakant Township have been newly displaced by clashes between the Burma Army and the Kachin Independence Army (KIA) in recent days, according to local leaders and Kachin rebel sources, who said that the fighting erupted after government forces entered a rebel-controlled ruby mining area.
Hla San, a National League for Democracy (NLD) member based in Hpakant town, said he visited the civilians who fled Sai Para village and were provided refuge in two churches in another village located east of Hpakant.
"They have been taking care of by our religious leaders.
One church has about <hide> people and another church has over <hide> people.
They ran away from their homes as they were afraid of the fighting," he said, adding that he and other Kachin leaders from Hpakant delivered food aid to the displaced villagers on Tuesday.

Daung Khar, a member of the technical consultancy team of the Kachin Independence Organization, the political wing of the KIA, said the rebels had clashed with the Burma Army last Friday and Sunday at Sabaw Maw, a ruby mine controlled by the KIA.
"According to the news we received, the government troops entered our area of control.
This is why our troops shoot at them," he said, adding that he lacked further details of the fighting.
The Kachin News Group reported that four Burmese soldiers were killed during the violence and that government troops seized a KIA base next to the ruby mine and burned it down.
The KIA has been fighting an insurgency against the Burma Army since mid-<hide> when a <hide>-year-old ceasefire broke down.
Since then, some <hide> civilians in Kachin and northern Shan states have been displaced.
Hostilities quieted down in February <hide>, but skirmishes have continued as a bilateral ceasefire between the sides has proven elusive.
In recent months, fighting in northern Burma has intensified and several thousand local civilians have been newly displaced.

Fighting has also spread to northern Shan State as government troops have increasingly fought with the Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA), an ally of the KIA that also lacks a ceasefire with Naypyidaw.
